description: Amazon Saheli Accelerate Season 2 Amazon India Amount Not specified Deadline Apr 15, 2026 Type accelerator Stage idea mvp early_revenue + 2 more Categories Social Impact Tags women accelerator amazon + 9 more About this Opportunity Amazon Saheli Accelerate Season 2 is a structured scale-up program run by Amazon India in partnership with DPIIT and Startup India. It selects 50 women-led businesses — nearly three times the first season's cohort — with a sharper focus on founders from Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities who are building or ready to build D2C brands online. The program centers on a three-day intensive in-person bootcamp, where participants receive hands-on mentorship from Amazon leaders, industry practitioners, and IIT-Delhi incubation faculty. Tracks cover digital marketing, financial management, brand building, and sustainability practices. Each participant is assigned a Saheli Associate who guides them through the full onboarding process on Amazon.in — including complimentary cataloguing, imaging, and listing support — removing the most common operational friction points for new sellers. Access to Amazon Seller University modules and peer learning circles runs throughout the program. It wraps up with a Demo Day where selected founders pitch directly to venture capitalists, creating a structured pathway to external funding for the most investor-ready businesses. There is no cash grant. The program's value lies in infrastructure access, mentorship quality, and the Amazon distribution channel — meaningful for founders who want to validate and grow a product business without needing to build a sales team from scratch. Eligibility Women-led or women-founded businesses — women must hold at least 51% ownership Open to cooperatives and self-help groups in addition to registered companies Businesses registered in India: sole proprietorship, partnership, LLP, or private limited company are all eligible Active sellers on Amazon.in or businesses planning to launch on the pl

grantTitle: MVP-Stage Startup Grants & Funding programs in India
description: MVP-stage startups qualify for BIRAC's BIG scheme (up to ₹50 Lakhs), SISFS grants, and state-level prototype development funds. These programs support technology de-risking, beta testing, and initial user acquisition, enabling founders to demonstrate proof-of-concept before approaching institutional investors.

grantTitle: Startup Grants & Funding programs in Kerala
description: Kerala's KSUM (Kerala Startup Mission) is one of India's most active state startup agencies, operating the IDEA Grants, Innovation Grants, and Scale-Up programs. Incubators at Technopark Trivandrum, Infopark Kochi, and MakerVillage provide dedicated hardware prototyping labs and seed funding up to ₹30 Lakhs.

grantTitle: Growth / PMF-Stage Startup Grants & Funding programs in India
description: Growth-stage startups with product-market fit benefit from BIRAC's LEAP scheme (up to ₹1 Crore), SIDBI's Fund of Funds for Startups, and PLI-linked manufacturing incentives. These programs support market expansion, technology scaling, and working capital needs for startups generating consistent revenue.

grantTitle: Grant Programs for Indian Startups
description: Non-dilutive government grants are the most founder-friendly source of startup capital in India. Programs like the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS), BIRAC BIG, DST NIDHI-PRAYAS, and iDEX provide ₹10 Lakhs to ₹1.5 Crore without equity dilution, enabling startups to validate technology and achieve product-market fit on their own terms.

grantTitle: Startup Grants & Funding programs in Punjab
description: Punjab's iStart Punjab initiative provides seed grants, mentor matching, and co-working access for startups across Mohali, Ludhiana, and Amritsar. The Punjab Infotech and IKG Punjab Technical University incubators support agri-tech, industrial automation, and logistics startups with dedicated prototyping infrastructure.

grantTitle: Idea-Stage Startup Grants & Funding programs in India
description: Idea-stage startups in India can access pre-revenue funding through DST's NIDHI-PRAYAS (up to ₹10 Lakhs), Atal Innovation Mission's AIC grants, and university-run incubator seed programs. These schemes fund concept validation, initial prototyping, and early customer discovery without requiring incorporation or revenue history.

grantTitle: Startup Grants & Funding programs in Karnataka
description: Karnataka is India's leading startup state, home to over 14,000 DPIIT-recognised ventures. KITS (Karnataka Innovation and Technology Society), the Elevate program, and the Karnataka Startup Cell offer grants from ₹20-50 Lakhs alongside dedicated Grand Challenge programs for civic-tech, health-tech, and agri-tech.

grantTitle: Seed Funding Programs for Indian Startups
description: Seed funding programs in India bridge the gap between bootstrapping and institutional venture capital. SIDBI Fund of Funds, SISFS, state-level startup seed funds, and BIRAC's LEAP scheme offer ₹5 Lakhs to ₹1 Crore in early capital alongside mentoring, helping pre-revenue startups reach revenue milestones and investor readiness.

description: Browse government schemes, CSR grants, accelerators and seed funding programs for Social Impact startups in India. Filter by stage, amount, and eligibility.

grantTitle: Incubation Programs for Indian Startups
description: India hosts over 900 government-recognised incubators under the Atal Innovation Mission, DST's NIDHI scheme, and MeitY's TIDE 2.0 program. Incubation support includes subsidised workspace, prototyping labs, regulatory navigation, and seed funding from ₹5-25 Lakhs for technology validation and early market testing.

grantTitle: Startup Grants & Funding programs in Tamil Nadu
description: Tamil Nadu operates StartupTN and the TANSIDCO Startup Fund, providing seed grants from ₹10-30 Lakhs alongside the TANSEED program. The state's incubation ecosystem — IIT Madras Research Park, IITM Incubation Cell, PSG-STEP, and Tidel Park — ranks among India's strongest for deep-tech and SaaS ventures.

grantTitle: Startup Grants & Funding programs in Haryana
description: Haryana's Enterprise Promotion Policy and the Hartron-backed iStart initiative fund startups across Gurugram, Faridabad, and Karnal. DPIIT-recognised startups in Haryana receive stamp duty exemption, incubation subsidies, and seed funding up to ₹10 Lakhs under the state's Startup Policy.

grantTitle: Early Revenue-Stage Startup Grants & Funding programs in India
description: Early-revenue startups with initial traction can access BIRAC's BIPP (up to ₹50 Lakhs equity-free), SIDBI's Samridhi Fund, and state-level growth grants. These programs fund customer acquisition, unit economics optimisation, and team building during the critical zero-to-one revenue phase.

description: Browse government schemes, CSR grants, accelerators and seed funding programs for Climate Tech startups in India. Filter by stage, amount, and eligibility.

grantTitle: Startup Grants & Funding programs in Gujarat
description: Gujarat's iCreate and the Gujarat Industrial Policy 2020 provide startup grants from ₹10-30 Lakhs alongside the Startup Gujarat initiative. The state houses over 50 government-recognised incubators, and the Student Startup and Innovation Policy (SSIP) 2.0 funds collegiate innovators from prototype to market.

grantTitle: Startup Events in India 2026 — Conferences, Hackathons & Networking
description: Discover upcoming startup events, conferences, hackathons, demo days, and networking sessions across India. Meet investors, founders, and grant funders — all in one place.

grantTitle: Scaling-Stage Startup Grants & Funding programs in India
description: Scaling-stage startups can access SIDBI's Growth Capital and Equity Assistance, EXIM Bank's export facilitation loans, and PLI production-linked incentives across 14 sectors. The Startup India initiative also provides tax exemptions under Section 80-IAC and self-certification under labour and environment laws to reduce compliance overhead.

grantTitle: Startup Grants and Funding programs for EV startups in India
description: Browse government schemes, CSR grants, accelerators and seed funding programs for EV startups in India. Filter by stage, amount, and eligibility.

grantTitle: Student-Founded Startup Grants & Funding programs in India
description: Student-founded startups are supported by the AICTE Student Startup and Innovation Policy (SSIP), Gujarat's SSIP 2.0, and Atal Innovation Mission's Atal Tinkering Labs. University incubators across IITs, IIMs, and NITs offer academic credit integration, pre-seed grants up to ₹10 Lakhs, and mentoring for collegiate founders building ventures during their studies.

grantTitle: Startup Grants & Funding programs in Maharashtra
description: Maharashtra's Startup Policy offers funding up to ₹15 Lakhs and hosts India's densest incubation network through IIT Bombay's SINE, Venture Center Pune, and the Maharashtra State Innovation Society (MSInS). Grants through the Chief Minister's Innovation Fund and sector-specific Grand Challenges in health, agri, and fintech attract startups from across the country.

grantTitle: Women-Led Startup Grants & Funding programs in India
description: Women-led startups receive dedicated funding through NITI Aayog's Women Entrepreneurship Platform (WEP), SIDBI's Stand-Up India scheme, Telangana's WE Hub, and BIRAC's WISER program. Multiple state startup policies reserve 20-30% of their seed fund corpus for women founders, and DST's KIRAN scheme supports women in STEM entrepreneurship.

grantTitle: DPIIT Registered Startup Grants & Funding programs in India
description: DPIIT-recognised startups unlock the full spectrum of Startup India benefits: SISFS seed funding, income tax exemption under Section 80-IAC, self-certification under labour and environment laws, and fast-tracked patent examination at 80% fee reduction. Recognition also qualifies startups for government e-marketplace (GeM) procurement preferences and the Fund of Funds managed by SIDBI.

grantTitle: Contest Programs for Indian Startups
description: Government innovation contests and Grand Challenges offer prize money and pilot deployment opportunities for Indian startups. Programs like the Smart India Hackathon, NITI Aayog's Atal New India Challenges, and MeitY's Digital India Awards provide ₹5-50 Lakhs in non-dilutive prizes alongside government procurement pathways.
